reviews.review_msg: Professor Liu is a gem - his lectures are clear and engaging. Stay on top of the material, use unstuck AI for extra prep, and you'll ace this class.

Junjie is one of the best econ profs at SFU/FIC. He teaches everything perfectly. He even tries to give quizzes which help to better understand the material taught. His accent might be a little problem for few but other than that there is no flaw. His tests are also quite easy and you just need to take a look on his notes to score well.

Of the worst classes I have had at Alexander College, Professor Junjie Liu only reads the slides, when grading only see the results and does not observe the procedures, makes evaluations with more than 20 formulas that must be memorized. His accent is very complicated to understand, for a course of this level the professors should have good diction
What a patient and great instructor at FIC, the school should hire more people like him! Excellent!
I skip so much class but also passed,I love you Junjie Liu. His class is fast and clear, easy for understand

He is pretty good prof with great (best) lectures. His teaching method is superb and his slides are more than enough to score well and understand concepts easily.
Quick tip: Never try to mug up concepts and try to complete all the questions!
Very nice instructor!
Best professor ever at FIC!!

It's really difficult to enroll in his classes. I guess that tells something. So I just waited patiently for a space to show up. I'm glad that I made the right decision. I did very well in midterms. I'm confident that I can do well in the final and get my first A at FIC.

I took Junjie's Econ 105 at FIC. His lecture slides are more than enough for you to do well in his class. He gives lots of practice questions. His exam questions are very similar to the practice questions.
Like his teaching style
Best Econ instructor at FIC!

After failing Econ 103 twice, I took the course again in 2023 spring semester. Junjie is very different from other 103 instructors at FIC. I should have passed the course if I took his course earlier.
Very good teacher, more than sufficient notes and clear grading scheme. If you read your notes thoroughly and do all weekly quizzes on time, good performance is guaranteed.

Junjie have quizzes for 2 chapters each week which are hard. He has very thick accent to understand and he simply reads off the slides. He doesn't teach in detail when needed. The most annoying thing is that his exams have very little time, most of the class missed questions every time. And he doesn't increases time for exam.
Junjie explains until you understand. His tests are easy as long as you study all of his PDFs.
Best economics teacher

The amount of instructional time that he provided was less than the amount required for the course. Implemented self-studying that's ineffective as Econ103 isn't a breeze of a course. He read off of his slides and taught blandly with not much detail and restricted the time limit on the midterms and final to only 1 hour. Would rate a 0 if possible.

He only reads off from slides so no need to attend lecture. Midterms and exams are way harder than the practices. He makes exams only 1 hour with 4 written questions (needs to upload) and 20 Mcqs with lots of math involved. No one finished the exam. Class average was around 59% so wouldn't recommend taking this class.
best prof, easy to get A if you read and understand his slides.

In class, he just read off the slides and did not teach, however, you don't really need to attend the class to know the materials. Everything he taught and everything in the exam can be easily countered by reading from his slides and working on MCQs. No need for textbook.

Don't take this class whatever you do. I studied a week straight for this class and still ended up doing really poor. He is a very hard marker, exams are confusing/make no sense, and he doenst event teach he just reads off of his lecture slides thats it. He makes no sense when he talks, and even the TAs agreed the exams were way too hard. Avoid!
He reads from the slides throughout the class and doesn't explain the material properly. It's basically useless to attend his lectures at all.

junjie is such an amazing prof! I took 103 with seong jun and got a c- and then took 105 with junjie and got a B+. His exams are super easy and literally same questions as practice. So much easier to get an A with junjie comparing to others. I def would recommend him
Kind and willing to answer students questions
Good.
Best Chinese Econ instructor everrrr.
Junjie is caring and helpful. Heartily wish your baby grow up happily and healthily Look forward to studying with you (if possible).
really informative powerpoint slides, you don't really need to attend the classes, curve grading, provided MCQ and tutorial question, hard to get A
BORING, reads off the slides, lectures are useless. not willing to help students with feedback. more useful to study at home with your textbook
Nice people,class not too hard.

All Junjie did was read off the slides, which was not helpful for people like me who needed more explanations behind Econ concepts. I had to rely on outside help and tutors. His tests were heavily math based with formulas and graphs. His accent made it a little hard to understand him too. One of the worst profs I've had in my 4 years here at SFU.

His lecture is kind of boring, but I need to say that he is a pretty nice teacher. In his class you can get an A even A+ easily, at least easier than other professors. What you need to do is previewing his PPT and making some notes about emphasis in advance. More importantly, do his practice certainly after his class and you will get at least B.

Junjie knows his stuff, but he does not know how to teach. He's been approached with questions numerous times but he doesn't know how to teach concepts in an effective way. He shows a lack of understanding for students who don't understand concepts fast.. It was a mistake taking a class with him.
Very boring class, dry material, and he just reads off his slides. Exams were pretty hard, would not take again.

Very organized and professional. His notes are clear and his exams are challenging, but straightforward. He was very helpful when I came to him privately for help and explained things very clearly. My only criticism is that not enough practice problems are provided and no textbook for ECON 290 to do further readings.

Junjie Liu has a really thick accent that is extremely hard to understand creating a barrier for a student to understand course material. He also relies on his powerpoints a lot when teaching. He would read off the slides and expand on his points minimally. I wish that I could have understood his better to receive a better grade.
I took his econ 103 and econ 105. I really love this professor. His notes are clear and his exams are not so hard because he will give you lots of MC exercises. It's easy to pass and get an A.
greatppt is clear and easy to understand. no textbook require. he is a really nice person!

Great prof, everything is straight forwards and there are no curve balls. Minimal weekly review and tutorial question completion is absolutely enough to get an A. His lectures sometimes get boring and you may stop paying attention but his slides are so amazing that you'll be fine nonetheless. Best prof I've had thus far, highly recommend his class.

Junjie is a great professor and he is always available for extra help. He basically put everything you need to know in the lecture slides, so the textbook is not really necessary. The final and midterms were very similar to the tutorial questions. Even some MCs were copied directly from the practice MCQs. Highly recommend Junjie for 105!
the best Chinese econ teacher ever
Junjie Liu is a nice prof because he will help me with talking Chinese language.  If you are not a Chinese, don't take this class please because his english is poor and not good enough to help you
Junjie is nice and willing to offer extra helps. The lectures were very organized and the exams were fair.

He encourages students to work hard and be more motivated. He cares about students a lot and will always be there for students. His speech is pretty clear. You have to do all the homework to get a pass and also the exam is pretty fair.

8:30am class and his monotone voice did not keep me awake. Coming from a non-business background, it was confusing. Felt like I got thrown into the world of Econ. Tests were hard; involved a lot of calculations and graphs. Lectures were confusing, he just reads off the slides. I learned more from Google than his lectures.

Junjie cares about his students. He encourages you to ask for help and has numerous questions available for practice. I found the two midterms to be fair however the final was significantly harder so be prepared. Do not need the text

Attend lectures even though he has the slides available online! During lecture he specifies what is important and what isn't, and goes through examples which aren't on the lecture slides. Do all the tutorial questions, and practice exams as they are similar to his actual exams! Very easy to beat the curve if you follow these instructions :)
avoid his class. all he does in class is just reading his notes which is very pretty boring. He doesn't speak english.  SFU shouldn't hire this guy
He's a cute prof.  Fun class.  Read his notes and you will pass his exams easily !!!!

Liu gives straightforward lectures. His slides are well corresponded with the textbook and serve as excellent review material. He is approachable and open to provide help. Exams are fair and you can do well if you take the time and effort to review.

Excellent notes, always available online. However, if you want answers to some of the questions in the notes, you will have to attend lectures. The lectures are a bit on the boring side, but the prof is always happy to help. Read the notes before and after class and you should be fine. Good luck!

HEADS UP: He does have an accent, but it's not that bad at all.   He is perhaps the most helpful prof I've had yet. He will answer any / all questions during the lecture, and sticks around for both the break and after lecture to answer any / all questions that have arisen. You will have nearly weekly assignments but they're simple + short!
Really helpful prof . He is clear and the test are not hard and easy to understand. Best prof at SFU.

One of the best professors this semester. Took both Econ 105 and 103 with him. His tests are straightforward, clear, and very, very similar to the practice exams and tutorial questions. Class average for both midterms around 75% and the course gets scaled. Accent isn't too bad, and you really get used to it after a while.

His class is very simple. No surprises whatsover. If you do and understand the weekly questions (which are usually only 4/week), you will excel. Notes are well-organized. Easy to do well, he is difficult to understand at times b/c of his accent but material in this class is easy enough so that it doesn't really matter.

Best Prof ever!! I've took his 105 290 282, his lecture is organized and his exams not that hard if you have done his tutorial questions, MC sample questions and understand them. He's patient and helpful, he is my favourite.
Good professor
Kind professor ever
so far so good

He is a nice teacher. Every time I ask him questions, he has the patient to help me. His lectures are well organized. If you do all his exercises, you will be fine in this course.  Anyway, he is the best teacher I have met in SFU so far.
He is a nice teacher. You will get a good grade if you do his exercise. His lectures are well organized. Best teacher ever!!!
He is so nice.  I have taken many his courses, ECON 103 282 105.  His notes include all the main points of textbook.
He speaks Chinglish with can not be understand by someone, exam is tough and final is really matter. He will deducted 2% from final grade for absent. Tough grader ever.

I would suggest you guys to buy past midterm papers as reference! It was very helpful, because he gave similar questions on the final too! So MAKE SURE you study ALL your midterm papers and the MC practice questions! GOOD LUCK everyone.
Pretty good prof, lecture slides are good enough if you don't want to spend a lot of time with this class. Midterms/Final were fair, class average surprisingly low as per usual

By far the nicest prof i have ever had. His midterms and finals are very hard but also EXTREMELY fair. Attend tutorials even though they are not mandatory because it is the only way you'll do well. He is extremely helpful and his midterms/finals are very similar to the practice ones
Lecture notes are really confused. Exams are totally different from lecture notes or tutorial questions.
ok
very nice prof, his notes are really helpful.
easy to pass the course, but extremely hard to get a good grade

His english skills are fine. Much better than average. His course notes are very thorough, but I'd rather a prof that tries to tie in real life present situations instead of just teaching out of the textbook. Study hard for the midterms and do well because the final is pretty brutal and you'll thank yourself later. The course is curved.

midterms were ok, the final was BRUTAL! i didn't even know why i had to study, because all the answers to the final were basically NOT from the textbook for anywhere! TAs are somewhat helpful. if you find a mistake on mt marking, don't even bother asking for that extra mark, cuz they won't give it even if u're right!
Does not speak English. 1st midterm tough but doable. 2nd midterm and final he went pedal to the metal. Avoid like the plague.

Midterms were fine, but the final was brutal. Marking on the final was rediculously hard and unfair, probably why the average was 50%. Waste of money, I would not take Econ 105 again with this professor.

midterms were okay, but final was brutal! its useless to study for the final because when u look at it, it seems like u don't even need to study for it, since it's so freakin hard! class avg didn't even reach 50%! u basically can skip all his lectures and study at home, cuz he copys everything from the textbook!

An average prof, his midterms were all right but his final was insanely difficult. Averages for both midterms were about 60-70% and the final was around 45%... His notes are basically the same as the text so you could study either one and do fairly well.

The textbook is a great read, it does a good job of explaining the concepts. His lecture notes are basically words taken from the textbook, but his notes do focus on the main points if you don't like studying from the textbook.  I thought midterms were relatively straightforward, too easy that he had to make the final harder (It was very brutal).

Either go over his notes really well, or read the textbook. Notes are short and and direct, but the book will provide you with more information and theory behind the economics- probably comes more handy during the tests. MTs are alright- first one had like 70+% average, Final is very brutal- much harder concepts, need to know EVERYHTING.

I like his lecture notes which clear and easy to understand and same as the textbook.Mt and final will kill you! specially 2 hours to write the final exam with 8 questions each of them have A,B,C parts. As I remembered 3 or 4 ppl left after 30 min of 2hr exam.. i guessed they done or give up..

Tried getting his help early in semester, never emailed me back. Went to all tutorials, did textbook questions and studied like mad, still only barely passed midterms because he throws curveball questions at you. Won't let you clarify questions in the exam, but his poor english cost the class grades on the 2nd midterm because of that. Ridiculous!
Class attendance is absolutely pointless since everything covered in the lecture comes directly from the textbook, even the examples were exactly the same as the ones in the text.

Once you get over his accent, his notes are easy, All notes are written on an overhead, so easy to follow. Hw is straight from the notes. Midterms are fair, final is tough if you dont study, only used the text to study for the final. Great guy

The notes are clear. Do not just rely on the notes, you have to read the textbook too. The midterms are very fair and very straight forward. The final exam was extremely hard. I don't know why people say he's not passionate in teaching, he wishes everyone a good weekend and good luck in studying.

A lot of people have time understanding him because of his accent, but it's not THAT bad. Besides, he writes everything he says on the overhead anyway. Lectures are dry and it is rare to get more insight into economics. Final is brutal and exam questions are weighed disproportionally- imo. If you don't understand him, use the text- it's the same.
His notes are really organized and clear, as long as you read the textbook and his notes, you'll do fine. The Final was brutal though but the midterms are same as his tut questions.

Very bad personality. Don't even try to talk with him. Don't even care about his students. If you fail, then it's all your problem. Has no passion on what he is teaching about. Don't like this person at all, even though I came from the same country as him.
This prof has no ability to teach at all. Needs to work on speaking English as you can't understand him at all. He brings his notes and copies it down without explanation. The worst prof ever.

Econ 282 is useless and open for making $? The class is Finance and even use 400 Finance textbook. Liu is nice BUT he knows NOTHING about Finance! Midterm 1 class avg below 50%. At least ONE mistake in each lecture note! I know cuz im 400 Finance student
This prof can't even speak English and copies down his lecture notes onto an overhead for us to copy his notes. What a waste of money by taking this course with a useless prof like Junjie Liu.
Seriously? His finals were SSOO hard. They had absolutely no relevance to what he said was going to be on the exam.
worst prof i ever had. end of story
good prof, close to his notes and questions on TUTOR OF TA, you will be great in this class. he is a good teacher with great and clear notes
he is a great prof, his exam is very close to your book and assignment. if you want to get good point, you should read the book and his question on tut
He's crystal clear, and extremely nice. His midterms are easy. All you have to do is study his notes and attend class!

Very straightforward Prof. Go to every lecture, do your study questions and refer to ur text book and you will be guranteed a B or better. Theres no better Econ prof in sfu. I mean how often can you learn stuff easily and still get a good mark.

The textbook he uses is very helpful for exams. His notes are right out of the textbook so if you read the short chs. you should do well on the exams. Recommend taking ECON103 prior to 105 with him. Grades are all based on 2MTs = 25% each and one final 50%. Overall, its an easy A-/B if you put in the time to learn the stuff.
Great prof! the lecture notes are very clear. As long as you understand and remember the notes, you are good for the exams. I never used my textbook.

All I got to say is he's impossible to argue with. TA's were much more helpful but the teaching staff for this course tends to pass around responsibility too much.For example, TA1 says "I agree, talk to prof for marks" Prof says, "Mmm I dont know, talk to TA2." TA2 says, "Only prof can change marks, talk to him."                          WWWWWWTFFF

he said his notes are enough for the course,its not true...went to every lecture and at the end i ended up photo copying  the textbook for detailed info or else...since his notes are extremely brief...dont even anticipate a good mark if you just study his notes. Final is extremely hard, some questions are gay that are not even taught in class.

I really liked my course with Junjie. He's very concise in his notes and he writes everything he says so you shouldn't miss anything in lecture. The exams were fair and his assignments were helpful. He won't work to get you interested but he will effectively teach you the material.

Junjie is a mediocre professor, with poor and unclear notes. I did all my learning from the textbook, and ended up doing fairly well in the class. His marking scheme is pedagogically limiting (2 midterms, 1 final), but the tutorials are helpful for actually learning the material. While Junjie is not a good professor, he's not necessarily a bad one.

If you read and understand all his notes and the textbook, you will do fine. Definitely helpful to attend tutorials since the TAs give out answers to weekly assignment questions.  Prof needs to improve on his English to make it easier for students to understand him.

If you read and understand everything about his notes and the textbook, you will do ok.  TAs give out answers for weekly questions in tutorials, definitely helpful. However, prof needs to improve his English so all students can understand him clearer.
An average prof. Not too bad, not too good. The bad thing about him is his marking scheme: two midterms 25% each, and one final for 50%

he is the typical asian prof. loves math, straight forward notes, teaches them as he copys it from his notes, EXTREMELY HIGH EXPECTATIONS ON MIDTERMS AND FINAL! hes gives great notes and easy textbook but he will ask you killer questions so memorization will not matter here. He tests you on your comprehension.
TERRIBLE PROF. he's unclear, terrible at answering questions, his T.A's are useless and he doesn't post ANYTHING. no syllabus, no answers to the tutorial questions and no notes. terrible class, AVOID
Hard to understand, material straight from the book-word for word. Tutorials not mandatory. Grades come from 2 midterms and exam.
fair MTs x2 , as long as you went to tutorial and did those practice problems. Final was a little bit tricky but in general a great prof. Must attend class, useful notes.

This guy is the most straight-forward prof you will ever get.  You probably won't use the textbook,, I sure didn't.. this class is very little work.. 2 midterms 25% each and final worth 50%.. 90% of the questions are from your NOTES..JunJie is a good man

Junjie is so calculation based that he cannot explain anything unless it is a math based problem.  He is so caught up in writing calculations in lecture and tutorial that he never answers questions.  Course difficulty, well I am getting a A in Econ 291 and all my other courses and getting a C- in his course. Nuff said...
2 mts were fair...final was difficult
Fair midterms, but pretty hard final. Notes are pretty good. In closing words, if I were to take another course with him, I would.
Good Professor~ Helpful and nice. Attend his lectures, copys the notes and understands!(Is a must) Fair midterm x2 and confusing final x 1.
Very Cute~ I like him~ Notes were extremely clear, two midterms were ok, BUT!! Final was extremely HARD!!!!! too hard...you should be careful for his final!! Otherwise, you will be in trouble.

I want to curse all the people who told me to take a course with him... Hard and confusing exams. Materials based on the notes, and the notes aren't that much of help for me. The course workload is like 'none'. You can't really do any practice in this course other than going over past midterms. Are you genius? then you can try his courses.
His exam is extremely challenging. I was in his tutorial in the summer semester, very bad experience. Even the T.A taught better then him. However, extremely low work load.
he is not as good as you think, hard exams, not recommended

If you get higher than a B+ for either Econ 103 or Econ 290, avoid this course. This course has minimum course load - two midterms and one final - but the materials are unclear and self-contradictory. Thought this was a GPA booster, but it ended up otherwise. AVOID Junjie with this course if you are good at  microeconomics.

Junjie's a nice prof. But he's definitely not  good at teaching/communicating his ideas during the lectures. All he does is simply regurgitating the things he read off the textbook. His notes are copy&paste from the textbook as well. I couldn't say for other courses but don't take this course with him. Spend your time/effort on something valuable.
His notes are really all you need for this course. No surprises on the midterm or final if you study those and do the assignments.

Junjie is an excellent professor whose lecture notes were clearly and concisely laid out each class. His midterms and final exam (although final was challenging) almost perfectly reflected what was taught in the lectures making it very easy to follow along during the sem. and study for exams. I'm taking another class w/ him b/c he's THAT good.

A nice teacher would like to devote lot of time to make his student clearify the course materials. his notes is very helpful, it save my money on my textbookA_A. midterm makes u feel u are the smartest person and final makes u like an idioit.

Junjie is friendly and his notes are exactly what he talks about in lectures. His notes are pretty much summaries of the text so if you understand the notes no need to read the book. His midterms were easy but his final was a killer.
I took both ECON 290 and 291 with him. He is goooooood!

Liu is a GOOD teacher. He comes to class and lectures with his well almost perfect notes and answers your questions somewhat well but go to him one on one and he will answer much better.  You need to just go to class and your grade will improve by a letter grade atleast. Liu is also very funny!!!

I have took 2 classes of him...he rocks!!!  well-organized and the mid-term and finalz are fair!!! no need to read the textbook coz his lecture notes is already clear enough!!  He is the best prof that i ever had!!!!!
A very good professor... Like him
Junjie is the BEST PROF EVER!!!
Junjie is by far the best prof at SFU. Very helpful and knowledgeable. I wish I have each and every class with him, he rocks. Gives the best notes and he is very friendly guy. You da man, Junjie.

He is the best professor at SFU!!  His notes are clear and easy to follow.  His lectures are interesting and informative.  Plus he is also very very good at explaining.  Junjie should definitely teach more courses at SFU!  This will make our lives much easier ^^
Pretty clear notes.  Mainly study for his notes and you will be fine.  However his theory on his exams are super hard.  You have really need to know ins and outs for the therory.

Notes are everything. Study the notes and you should be fine. Not too much math, mostly theory form notes (75%theory, 25%math).  Hard to understand sometimes during lecture. Didn't find him very helpful during office hours.
good prof, bad english, note: Lecture NOTES are everything, 2/3 on theories, 1/3 math,

Organized, easy-going and helpful prof. The lecture notes he sent u are hard to understand if u dun attend lectures. make sure u focus on the notes while studying for exams. The tutorial questions are almost completely different from the exams! Lucky enough that midterms are fairly easy, but final exam is very tough.

Screw the Textbook! The textbook will confuse you, only focus on his notes. Liu is better than the other two 301 profs (Martin & Tchkinov), at least Liu won't treat this as a math class, he focus less on calculus and a lot more on theories. Liu's exams are also the most unexpected out of my 3 yrs in SFU. Liu's "B" range and "C" range is very large.

Avg for MTs are 61-64%, grades on curve. Junjie, good prof, but hard to understand w/ his accent. TA YiLin was sort of BETTER. Material is NO DOUBT HARD. Junjie, not a big calculus person, there was still calculations, but NO TRICKY calculas, THANK GOD. Knows his stuff very well.

A really nice prof with a thorough understanding of the course, but his exams were tough!  His final tested much more theory than his calculations-based midterms. If you ever go and see him during office hours, he is always happy to help. Do not skip his lectures, you need his notes!
His notes are very clear and detailed. Midterms were fair but final exam was tough. Over 80% the final exam was on lump-sum and per-unit stuff. So memorize it.

hard...tough, I am still in this class, the mids are hard, but never know why the class avg are still so high for 2 mids. the mids are tricky, past mids and final are useless, Jliu will just change very much for every possible questions. So dun trying to memorize his format of testing you guys! That's all!!! good luck
He's a really nice guy. I had to deal with him personally a few times and he was always accomodating. Make sure you can do the comparative statics with the models, don't worry about the math.

Yeah, his english isn't good, but its perfectly understandable. As long you pay attention in class & take good notes you'll be fine. I found the textbooks & tutorials to be purely optional - just make sure u completely understand his lecture notes. And do all his past exams. I got an relatively easy A by doing that, and I'm not even an ECON major.
Not bad. Try not to fall asleep, but don't rely soley on lectures, you have to force yourself to read this, cuz it's pretty dry. The actual prof tries, but, man.. boring.
Typical ESL grad student doing lectures. Very responsive to suggestions and questions. If you prefer algebra over calculus, take the class with him. Otherwise, stick with any of the other lecturers.
organized notes.  after take 301, 305 is easy taking with him.  fair midterms and pretty hard finals but still worth a take.  but he definitely need to improve his english.

His english do need improvement, lots "and","so"... Lecture is not interesting, but still understandable. Still, his exam is relative fair. I hate too easy exams in other econ courses, that everyone got grades not depending on performance but TA. Recommand: DO practice or past exams.
Lectures weren't the greatest, but you could pick anyhting up in the text.  If you'd taken 301 before, this class was EASY.
Let's just say he sux at teaching.
he spends too much time lecturing on easie materials but too little time on challenging materials (like IS-LM stuff) ... overall fair exams and informative lectures.
Lectures were alright, but exams were fair and he's extremely helpful in office hours.

lectures are not that clear, he's a nice guy but just have problems getting his point through to students. exams were fair if u actually get the material however, with his teaching, u might not. stutters like porky pig once in a while..

The WORST instructor. Fails to explain his materials in class, and  gives hard midterms + finals! u need to be very good at math to pass his course.  His notes are too simple and needs elaboration!  TERRIBLE EXPERIENCE!!!!
Lack of english skill. U better be good at math if u want to pass his exam .. u'll know what i mean~

VERY unorganized notes, lack of english skills hindered his ability to communicate material to students, made material more difficult than it actually is, tough exams, an average of 45% on the final should reflect his teaching ability
Very messy notes.  Sometimes, don't know where is where.  Hard to understand what he said on the class.
a nice teacher that gives out hard exams
very nice guy...
O.K communication. doesn't use a lot of application examples in his notes. hard to understand, hard midterm and final. Uses examples from text (tutorial). overall pretty diffcult course.
His midterms and exams are quite close to his past paper and he is very nice and helpful.
No Comments
notes are clear to me. He is very nice.
nice prof, unclear lecture
Not very good English but he's patient.His exam is from homework. Nice guy.
Lecture notes are terrible, unorganized, unclear, and hard to follow.
nice guy but bad lec notes and tough tests
Very unclear notes, unable to explain, but he is nice
